title: Star Wars Journal: The Fight for Justice
content:
{{Top|rwm|reb|italics=1}} {{Book |image=[[File:FightforJustice.jpg]] |title=''Star Wars Journal:
The Fight for Justice'' |author=[[John Peel]] |cover artist=[[Maren]] |illustrator= |editor= |publisher=[[Scholastic]] |early release= |release date=[[July]] [[1998]]''[[Star Wars Journal: The Fight for Justice]]'' |intl release= |rereleased= |media type=Paperback |pages=128 |isbn=0590511688 |isbn2= |isbn3= |timeline=[[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]]–[[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]]''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' |series=''[[Star Wars Journal]]'' |preceded by=[[Star Wars Journal: Captive to Evil|''Captive to Evil'']] |followed by=[[Star Wars Journal: Hero for Hire|''Hero for Hire'']] }} '''''Star Wars Journal: The Fight for Justice''''' is a [[Star Wars Legends|Legends]] junior novel written by [[John Peel]]. It is part of the ''[[Star Wars Journal]]s'' series, and it was published by [[Scholastic]] in [[July]] [[1998]], in conjunction with ''[[Star Wars Journal: Captive to Evil]]'' and ''[[Star Wars Journal: Hero for Hire]]''. ''The Fight for Justice'' is presented as a group of [[in-universe]] [[Datapad/Legends|datapad]] entries recorded by [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] in [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]]–[[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]], during the events of [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']]. ==Publisher's summary== ''SKYWALKER DATA PAD ENTRY'' ''LOCATION: TATOOINE'' ''In just half a day, my whole world has changed. The farm, my aunt and uncle.... They're gone. Destroyed by the Empire. All my life I've been waiting for something to happen to me. Something important. And now that it has, I'm not sure I know how to handle it.'' ==Plot summary== {{Plot}} ==Continuity== The book states that while searching for the runaway [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] in the [[Jundland Wastes/Legends|Jundland Wastes]], Luke Skywalker brought along a [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistol]] for protection. This is contradictory to the film, in which Skywalker brings a [[Slugthrower/Legends|slugthrower]]. The also book states that [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] cut off [[Cornelius Evazan/Legends|Cornelius Evazan's]] arm; in the film, Kenobi cuts off [[Ponda Baba/Legends|Ponda Baba's]]. As Skywalker and [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] fight to escape Imperial [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] aboard the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]], Skywalker pulls a rope and [[Grappling hook/Legends|grappling hook]] from his belt. Originally seen in ''A New Hope'', this rope was described as part of a stormtrooper's [[Utility belt/Legends|utility belt]]. However, the novel details the rope as one formerly used by Skywalker to pull [[Droid/Legends|droids]] out of sinkholes on Tatooine. The book states that [[Gold Leader/Legends|Gold Leader]] was able to fire off his [[Proton torpedo/Legends|proton torpedoes]] during [[Gold Squadron (Rebel Alliance)/Legends|Gold Squadron's]] attack run at the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]]. This is contradictory to the film, in which Gold Leader is shot down by [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] before being able to fire. [[General/Legends|General]] [[Jan Dodonna/Legends|Jan Dodonna]] is mistakenly referred to as an [[Admiral/Legends|admiral]]. ==Media== {{Mediacat|from=1|imagecat=Images from Star Wars Journal: The Fight for Justice}} ===Editions=== *{{ISBN|9780590189026}}; [[July]] [[1998]]; [[Scholastic]]; US paperback *{{ISBN|9780590511681}}; 1998; Scholastic Inc.; US paperback, school market edition{{Amazon|url=Star-Journal-Fight-Justice-Skywalker/dp/0590511688|text=Star Wars Journal The Fight for Justice by Luke Skywalker}} ===Cover gallery=== File:FightforJustice.jpg|US paperback
